news.title: NGT forms committee to investigate encroachments at Shahpura lake in Bhopal
news.para: The National Green Tribunal has formed a committee to investigate alleged encroachments and illegal constructions around Bhopal's Shahpura lake. The tribunal is concerned about untreated water discharge, obstructed drainage, and the lake's deteriorating water quality, which is unsuitable for use. This action follows a petition highlighting the ecological damage despite ongoing beautification efforts.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh government speeds up LRS clearances ahead of April 23 deadline
news.para: The Andhra Pradesh government is pushing to approve layout regularisation applications quickly. This scheme aims to bring unauthorized layouts into the legal framework. The deadline is approaching next week. Officials are directed to clear eligible applications without delay. This initiative seeks to boost state revenue and promote planned urban development. Over 61,000 applications have been received.

news.title: Maharashtra to roll out 60 private-run property registration centres from July
news.para: Maharashtra's registration department is launching 60 private centres by July, modelled on passport offices, to speed up property registrations. These centres will offer enhanced amenities for a processing fee of up to Rs 5,217, operating alongside existing government offices.

news.title: Gurugram to launch doorstep demolition waste pickup, tighten site inspections
news.para: Gurugram's MCG is launching door-to-door construction and demolition waste collection and enhancing disposal methods. This initiative, alongside traffic management and dust control measures, aims to reduce the city's Air Quality Index to 170 by 2026. The civic body is also addressing legacy waste at Bandhwari landfill.

news.title: Telangana HC gives government one week to file counters in GHMC merger case
news.para: The Telangana High Court has granted the state government a final week to file its counter-affidavits challenging the merger of panchayats, municipalities, and municipal corporations into GHMC. Petitioners, including a farmer whose farmhouse was recently demolished, argue the amendments are unconstitutional and arbitrary. The court also extended the status quo on demolitions in a specific case.

news.title: Missing papers put 2.5 lakh property deals in Gurugram on radar
news.para: The Income Tax department is investigating 2.5 lakh property deals in North India valued at over Rs 3 lakh crore. This action follows failures by revenue offices in Gurgaon and Faridabad to report transactions accurately. Missing or incorrect PAN details hampered tax verification. Surveys revealed significant tax evasion.

news.title: Govt to start demolition drive in Gurugram after Punjab & Haryana HC stays S+4 policy, DLF-1 first
news.para: Gurgaon authorities are launching a time-bound crackdown on illegal house extensions and misuse of stilt floors in licensed colonies, starting with DLF-1. This action follows a Punjab and Haryana High Court stay on the stilt-plus-four-floor policy due to infrastructure concerns. Residents are urged to voluntarily remove encroachments before enforcement teams begin demolitions.

news.title: Delhi: MCD readies SWAGAM portal for regularisation of 1,511 unauthorised colonies
news.para: The Municipal Corporation of Delhi's SWAGAM portal is nearing public launch for the regularisation of 1,511 unauthorised colonies. Drone surveys are underway to map properties. Residents will soon apply for regularisation through the simplified portal. Ownership rights under PM-UDAY are expected within a month, followed by application processing. This move will pave the way for infrastructure upgrades in these colonies.

news.title: Andhra Pradesh CM announces ₹40,000 lease per acre under Amaravati phase II land pooling
news.para: Andhra Pradesh Chief Minister N Chandrababu Naidu announced a Rs 40,000 per acre lease for a decade to farmers participating in Amaravati's second phase of land pooling.  The offer includes an annual Rs 3,000 hike and a Rs 1.5 lakh crop loan per household. This initiative aims to secure additional land for the developing capital city.

news.title: Nashik civic body to launch drive to check rainwater harvesting systems
news.para: The Nashik Municipal Corporation is launching an inspection drive to ensure rainwater harvesting systems are functional in buildings of 500sqm or more. Mayor Himgauri Aher instructed officials to review existing systems and consider penalties for non-compliance. Incentives for reporting violations and voluntary installations are also being explored to improve groundwater levels.

news.title: Lucknow development body launches OTS for around 4,000 defaulters
news.para: The Lucknow Development Authority (LDA) is launching a One-Time Settlement (OTS) program to aid approximately 4,000 defaulters. This initiative offers relief from penalties and compound interest, allowing allottees to regularize ownership by clearing dues. Applications will be processed within three months, with a 3% rebate for full payments made within 30 days.

news.title: Surat: Pragya Nagar won’t be charged property tax
news.para: Surat Municipal Corporation has waived property tax for residents of Pragya Nagar after their homes were demolished in 2025. The civic body took this step after residents received tax bills for the upcoming year, despite their colony no longer existing.

news.title: GVMC initiates comprehensive slum survey across Visakhapatnam
news.para: The Greater Visakhapatnam Municipal Corporation (GVMC) is conducting a comprehensive survey of 66 slums to assess development levels. Slums meeting certain criteria will be denotified, while others, like Velampeta and Indira Nagar, will undergo redevelopment through a public-private partnership. This initiative aims to transform the city into a slum-free zone with new high-rise residential apartments.

news.title: Sand shortage in Mangaluru adds ₹2 lakh to cost of building a house
news.para: Mangaluru builders face a severe sand shortage, escalating construction costs by nearly Rs 2.25 lakh per standard house. A four-year ban on sand mining in CRZ areas forces contractors to use coarser, less ideal sand from non-CRZ regions, leading to significant wastage. This crisis is pushing up prices and impacting project budgets.

news.title: MMRDA starts land acquisition for ‘Third Mumbai’; consent process from April 27
news.para: MMRDA has begun land acquisition for 'Third Mumbai,' the Karnala-Sai-Chirner New Town, spanning 124 villages in Raigad. Landowners can opt for monetary compensation or land-based returns, including a land pooling model. This initiative aims to boost regional economic growth and establish a major technology and infrastructure hub.

news.title: Over 900 acres of Mhada layouts will be redeveloped across Mumbai
news.para: Mhada is set to redevelop a dozen layouts spanning 923 acres, aiming to rehabilitate over 75,000 residents within 5-10 years. Key projects like Charkop and Gorai layouts will soon seek state cabinet approval. The initiative also includes slum rehabilitation and a push for rental housing under the state housing policy.

news.title: Bombay HC says Mhada could use unclaimed Patra Chawl flats for public housing
news.para: Bombay High Court has set a deadline of April 30, 2026, for Goregaon Siddharth Nagar Sahakari Griha Nirman Sanstha Limited society members to take possession of their tenements. If members fail to do so, the court will allow MHADA to utilize these flats for public housing. Rehabilitation buildings are ready for occupation.

news.title: Repair in 30 days, consumer panel tells Supertech after seepage in Greater Noida flat
news.para: A district consumer commission has found Supertech Limited guilty of deficiency in service for a flat in Ecovillage-2 that suffered from seepage and sewage problems. The builder was ordered to rectify the defects within 30 days and pay the owner Rs 4,000 for mental anguish and litigation costs.

news.title: NOC must for works within 20 meter radius of Pune metro tunnel
news.para: Maha Metro now requires builders and property owners to get a No Objection Certificate before starting any construction within 20 meters of Metro structures. This rule aims to protect the Metro's structural integrity. Failure to comply will result in legal action and financial penalties. The directive follows an incident where excavation damaged a Metro tunnel.

news.title: Mumbai: Four bidders in fray for BMC’s ₹1,348 crore Lower Parel plot
news.para: The BMC is nearing the appointment of a developer for a prime six-acre Lower Parel plot, valued at Rs 1,348 crore. Four major real estate players have submitted bids after the Supreme Court ruled in the BMC's favor, ending a long-standing dispute. The developer will be responsible for rehabilitating existing occupants on the 'as-is' basis.
